claim: Abenomics' combined monetary-fiscal expansion lifted Japanese inflation and output partially but failed to durably escape the deflation equilibrium, consistent with NK models of near-permanent ZLB traps.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Abenomics' combined monetary-fiscal expansion lifted Japanese inflation and output partially but failed to durably escape the deflation equilibrium, consistent with NK models of near-permanent ZLB traps.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Phase 4C candidate: hypothesis lacks sample.countries list — needs countries=[JPN] and period=[2012, 2020] explicitly added to hypothesis scope to resolve GEO_UNVERIFIABLE flag.

claim: Argentine convertibility and subsequent collapse (Menem 1991–2001) reflects rule-based money's appeal combined with fixed-regime rigidity risk that Austrian theory predicts.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Argentine convertibility and subsequent collapse (Menem 1991–2001) reflects rule-based money's appeal combined with fixed-regime rigidity risk that Austrian theory predicts.

Menem's 1991–2001 currency board succeeded at controlling inflation while rigid, but collapsed when fiscal profligacy at provincial level produced debt-service costs the fixed regime could not absorb — vindicating the Austrian warning that rule-based money cannot substitute for fiscal discipline.

Milei's 2023–2024 fiscal-first shock therapy (eliminating primary deficit, ending central bank deficit monetisation) will show faster inflation stabilisation than any prior Argentine programme that tried price controls or heterodox stabilisation first.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Austrian prediction: removing the fiscal-dominance root produces inflation decline within 12-24 months. Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope is Milei 2023-2024 disinflation but current hypothesis (argentina_default_collapse_output_effects) tests Menem 1991-2001 convertibility collapse. Phase 4C: candidate for relink to milei_reforms_reduce_argentine_inflation (better era/entity match) or new hypothesis milei_fiscal_first_disinflation_vs_heterodox_predecessors.

claim: Australian fiscal expansion during 2008-2009 GFC response prevented recession entry, consistent with MMT claim that currency-issuer fiscal space in high-unemployment regimes produces output gains with minimal inflation cost.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Australian fiscal expansion during 2008-2009 GFC response prevented recession entry, consistent with MMT claim that currency-issuer fiscal space in high-unemployment regimes produces output gains with minimal inflation cost.

claim: Pinochet-era Chile's monetary stabilisation (post-1975, advised by Chicago Boys) produced lower inflation and higher growth than contemporaneous Latin American countries using heterodox stabilisation.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Pinochet-era Chile's monetary stabilisation (post-1975, advised by Chicago Boys) produced lower inflation and higher growth than contemporaneous Latin American countries using heterodox stabilisation.

claim: Erhard's 1948 currency reform and price-control removal produced the fastest recovery in post-war Europe, demonstrating that rules-based market restoration outperforms Bretton-Woods-era rationed economies.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Erhard's 1948 currency reform and price-control removal produced the fastest recovery in post-war Europe, demonstrating that rules-based market restoration outperforms Bretton-Woods-era rationed economies.

claim: Over 50+ year horizons since the 1971 collapse of Bretton Woods, major fiat currencies (USD, GBP, EUR legacy components, JPY, AUD) have lost substantial purchasing power against hard assets (gold, broad commodity baskets, residential real estate). This is documented fact; the hypothesis re-establishes the pattern using provenanced public data.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Fiat currencies lose purchasing power against hard assets over long horizons.

Pre-1914 classical-gold-standard episodes (excluding wartime suspensions) show lower long-run average inflation than any comparable-length fiat-regime sample, even if short-run price-level volatility is higher.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Austrian steelman: long-run purchasing-power stability matters more than short-run volatility. Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope is pre-1914 classical-gold-standard era but current hypothesis (fiat_expansion_erodes_currency_purchasing_power_long_run) tests post-1971 fiat era. Phase 4C: candidate for new hypothesis classical_gold_standard_vs_fiat_long_run_inflation_comparison or claim rewrite to post-1971 framing.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Volcker disinflation shows that well-managed fiat with CB independence can restore price stability without a gold standard.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: NK accepts long-run purchasing-power erosion but denies it implies gold-standard superiority. Phase 4C candidate: claim tests Volcker disinflation / CB independence restoring price stability; linked hypothesis tests post-1971 fiat purchasing-power erosion vs hard assets. Entity/outcome mismatch — relink to central_bank_independence_inflation_discipline or new volcker_disinflation_effect hypothesis.

claim: Fossil-fuel subsidies across OECD 1980-2020 persisted despite declared climate commitments, consistent with the eco-socialist claim that private fossil-reserve ownership structurally blocks adequate public action.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Fossil-fuel subsidies across OECD 1980-2020 persisted despite declared climate commitments, consistent with the eco-socialist claim that private fossil-reserve ownership structurally blocks adequate public action.

claim: The 1929-1933 Great Depression contraction in US output was precipitated by an endogenous over-accumulation crisis in the 1920s (rising capital-to-output ratio, falling profitability) rather than by Federal Reserve policy errors alone.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: The 1929-1933 Great Depression contraction in US output was precipitated by an endogenous over-accumulation crisis in the 1920s (rising capital-to-output ratio, falling profitability) rather than by Federal Reserve policy errors alone.

claim: Every documented hyperinflation episode since 1900 (Weimar Germany, post-WW2 Hungary, Yugoslavia 1990s, Zimbabwe 2000s, Venezuela 2010s-2020s, among others) was preceded by fiscal dominance — a state of affairs where monetary policy is subordinated to financing government deficits that cannot be financed by taxation or market-rate borrowing. Monetary mismanagement alone, absent fiscal dominance, has not produced hyperinflation in the historical record.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Hyperinflation requires fiscal dominance as root cause.

Zimbabwe's 2000–2008 land-reform-plus-monetisation episode produced hyperinflation because the central bank monetised fiscal deficits once property-right expropriation collapsed the tax base — an instance of fiscal dominance.
Hyperinflations always involve fiscal dominance (monetary accommodation of deficits).
Zimbabwe's 2000–2008 hyperinflation involved monetary accommodation of fiscal deficits by a central bank that had lost operational independence, matching the Friedman-Schwartz pattern.

Zimbabwe's 2000-2008 hyperinflation originated in the fiscal-military crisis following land reform rather than in purely monetary expansion, consistent with Marxian emphasis on production relations as primary.
Hyperinflation requires fiscal dominance AND collapsed real supply / external-FX obligation — not monetary expansion alone.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: MMT accepts fiscal-dominance necessary condition; disputes that monetary growth alone is sufficient.

claim: Japanese public debt crossing 150%, then 200%, then 250% of GDP 1990-2020 did not trigger a solvency or inflation crisis, contradicting household-debt-analogue framings.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Japanese public debt crossing 150%, then 200%, then 250% of GDP 1990-2020 did not trigger a solvency or inflation crisis, contradicting household-debt-analogue framings.

claim: The 2020-2021 US fiscal response (CARES + ARP, roughly $5tn) produced a transient inflation episode that receded by 2023-2024 as supply-side capacity normalised, consistent with MMT's real-constraint view.

claim: Zimbabwe's Fast Track Land Reform Programme (FTLRP, 2000-2002) combined with Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe deficit monetisation produced a canonical institutional and economic collapse 2000-2009 that manifests as >=7 of 10 pre-registered extreme-outcome metrics, each drawn from an independent data source and measuring a different causal layer (agricultural-capacity destruction, monetary collapse, output contraction, human-capital flight, humanitarian stress). The canonical-case claim is that no non-war peacetime sub-Saharan African economy in the 2000-2009 window matches even 4 of these 10 thresholds simultaneously; Zimbabwe matches most. A refutation (<=3 metrics met) would indicate that Zimbabwe's collapse has been substantially overstated or that the 1980-1999 pre-reform trend was already trending toward collapse independent of FTLRP and RBZ monetisation.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Zimbabwe 2000-2008 hyperinflation followed the real-supply collapse from land reform and external sanctions; currency expansion was downstream of real-capacity destruction, not causally independent.

claim: Zimbabwean property-rights deterioration post-2000 (commercial-farm expropriation without compensation) precedes hyperinflation and output collapse; institutional mechanism is necessary, not merely monetary.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Zimbabwean property-rights deterioration post-2000 (commercial-farm expropriation without compensation) precedes hyperinflation and output collapse; institutional mechanism is necessary, not merely monetary.
